reasoning: Aravindh Chithambaram was ranked #11 in the April 2025 FIDE list with a rating of 2749. A detailed look at the rankings reveals a significant asymmetry around his position: he is closely trailing the top 10 (World #10 Alireza Firouzja and #9 Ian Nepomniachtchi are at 2757, an 8-point gap), but he is being tightly pursued by #12 Wesley So (2748), #13 Shakhriyar Mamedyarov (2748), and #14 Levon Aronian (2747). Because the gap below him is only 1-2 points, even a single draw against a lower-rated player or a minor rating gain by any of So, Mamedyarov, or Aronian in active tournaments would push Aravindh out of the top 11. Consequently, there is a much higher probability of him dropping to #12 or below than climbing to #10. Given normal rating volatility and this squeezed position, he is more likely than not to rank >11 in the upcoming FIDE May list. As the forecast horizon increases to July and October, uncertainty naturally reverts closer to 0.5 because he could establish a higher baseline or fall further down the pack.
